pricing.title: Plans for students, makers, and growing teams
pricing.description: Start simple, unlock deeper AI workflows, and scale GraphNode across your team when you are ready.

teamInquiry.title: Need a plan for your organization?
teamInquiry.description: If you need onboarding support, custom integrations, or a team-wide rollout plan, reach out and we will help shape the right GraphNode setup.

starter.name: Starter
starter.description: A solid starting point for personal workflows and everyday knowledge capture.

pro.name: Pro
pro.description: For power users who want deeper AI workflows and faster day-to-day execution.

max.name: Max
max.description: For advanced builders who want the fastest experience and the broadest model access.

team.name: Team
team.description: For teams rolling out GraphNode across workflows, members, and internal systems.

hero.tagline2: Designed for Better Learning and Productivity
hero.eyebrow: A more alive way to think, write, and connect ideas
hero.description: GraphNode brings markdown notes, AI chat, and graph visualization into one cinematic workspace so your ideas stay connected instead of buried.
hero.previewSignal: linked to notes, chats, and graph nodes
hero.chatDescription: Prompt the model with linked notes, connected nodes, and previous discussions without manually gathering everything first.
hero.flowTitle: A workspace that reacts while you think
hero.flowDescription: Animated states, layered surfaces, and graph motion make the product feel active instead of static.

features.title: Powerful Features
features.description: Instead of a flat utility page, each surface is designed to communicate momentum, structure, and clarity.

markdownNote.title: Markdown Notes
markdownNote.description: Record and organize your ideas with a powerful markdown editor
markdownNote.detail: Designed for everything from quick notes to longer documents without losing structure.

aiChat.title: AI Chat
aiChat.description: Chat with an intelligent AI assistant powered by OpenAI and DeepSeek
aiChat.detail: Ask questions with note and graph context already in place so the next step is faster.

graphVisualization.title: Graph Visualization
graphVisualization.description: Visualize your knowledge graph in 2D/3D by connecting notes and chats
graphVisualization.detail: Turn relationships into a visible structure so larger patterns are easier to understand.

smartSearch.title: Smart Search
smartSearch.description: Quickly search all notes and chats to find the information you need
smartSearch.detail: Return to the right note, thread, or concept quickly instead of digging through separate tools.

agent.title: Agent
agent.description: Automate various tasks with the AI assistant
agent.detail: Reduce repetitive work like summarizing and organizing so you can focus on execution.

sync.title: Sync
sync.description: Continue your work across devices with cloud sync
sync.detail: Carry the same workspace context across devices so your flow does not break.

workflow.title: A workflow where ideas stay visible, connected, and ready to act on
workflow.description: Each step is arranged as one continuous flow so ideas stay visible, connected, and ready to move into action.

capture.title: Capture
capture.text: Ideas, notes, references, and AI conversations stay in one graph instead of scattering across apps.

connect.title: Connect
connect.text: Link documents and conversations into a visible structure so relationships become easier to explore.

execute.title: Execute
execute.text: Turn context into actions with AI agents, fast retrieval, and a workspace that feels alive while you use it.

productProof.title: Real product capabilities, turned into an interactive story
productProof.description: I pulled these themes directly from the sibling front-end and backend repositories so the homepage reflects what GraphNode actually does.

visualize.label: Visualize
visualize.title: Graph exploration that opens notes and ideas as one connected map
visualize.description: Notes, chats, and concepts become nodes and relations, so you can follow summaries and nearby links without losing your place.
points: See a focus node and surrounding relations at the same time
Move through large graphs without losing orientation
Open subclusters to continue drilling into detail

microscope.label: Microscope
microscope.title: A deep workspace for turning fragments into findings
microscope.description: Selected notes and conversations can be grouped into one analysis run, then organized into outputs that lead into next actions.
points: Bundle notes and chat slices into one analysis run
Group outputs into browsable workspace results
Send selected findings directly into agent work

agent.label: Agent
agent.title: An AI agent that acts on selected context
agent.description: Instead of copying context into a blank prompt, you can pass notes, graph selections, and analysis results straight into the workflow.
points: Ask with attached sources already in context
Run quick actions like summaries and note creation
Keep answers and resulting artifacts in one flow

searchSync.label: Search + Sync
searchSync.title: A living knowledge base where search and sync stay in step
searchSync.description: Find notes, threads, and folders quickly, then keep changes moving across devices with recency-aware synchronization underneath.
points: Search across notes and conversations together
Move with a shortcut-first discovery flow

pipeline.title: A homepage section that now reflects the backend pipeline too
pipeline.description: The backend already supports queued graph generation, summaries, microscope analysis, agent tools, and synchronization, so the landing page now tells that story clearly.

collect.title: Collect
collect.text: Conversation data and markdown notes are gathered, streamed, and prepared as graph-generation input.

queue.title: Queue
queue.text: Graph generation, graph summary, and microscope ingest are orchestrated through SQS-backed background jobs.

enrich.title: Enrich
enrich.text: Semantic search, graph stats, clusters, subclusters, and agent tools turn raw content into navigable structure.

sync.title: Sync
sync.text: Notes, folders, conversations, and messages are synchronized with transaction-aware LWW conflict handling.

platforms.title: Download by Platform
platforms.description: Choose your platform and jump into a more animated, more visual GraphNode experience.
platforms.macosDescription: Optimized for Apple Silicon and Intel with a native desktop feel.
platforms.windowsDescription: Fast install, polished interface, and smooth graph interaction on Windows.
platforms.linuxDescription: Linux support is on the roadmap and will arrive with a dedicated release.
platforms.mobileDescription: A dedicated mobile experience is planned so you can browse and continue your graph anywhere.

team.title: TACO 4th Gen - GraphNode Team
team.subtitle: A team pursuing better learning and productivity through knowledge graphs

projectIntro.title: About the Project
projectIntro.paragraph1: GraphNode is a desktop application for visualizing and managing knowledge graphs. It provides note-taking, AI chat, and graph visualization all in one platform.
projectIntro.paragraph2: Started as a TACO (Tsinghua AI and Computer Organization) 4th generation project, GraphNode aims to help users intuitively understand and manage complex knowledge structures.
projectIntro.paragraph3: Built with Electron and React, it supports cross-platform use across various operating systems.

mcp.title: MCP (Model Context Protocol)
mcp.description: MCP is a protocol that allows AI assistants to extend their capabilities by connecting to external tools and data sources. When you connect an MCP server in GraphNode, AI can access file systems, databases, external APIs, and more.

customMcp.title: Create and Register Custom MCP Server
customMcp.description: To create your own MCP server, start by creating a Node.js project.

addMethod.title: How to Add Custom MCP Server
addMethod.description: You can add custom servers after enabling Settings → MCP Servers → Developer Mode.

github.title: GitHub MCP Server
github.description: MCP server for accessing GitHub repositories.
github.tokenLinkText: See the official documentation for detailed instructions.

sqlite.title: SQLite Database MCP
sqlite.description: MCP for accessing local SQLite databases.

puppeteer.title: Puppeteer (Web Automation) MCP
puppeteer.description: MCP for web page scraping and automation.

api.title: API Reference
api.description: GraphNode SDK is the official client library for TypeScript/JavaScript developers. It provides cookie-based session authentication and comprehensive API coverage.
api.sdkModulesDesc: The SDK consists of 8 main modules.

me.title: User (client.me)
me.description: User-related features including profile, API keys, and language settings

graph.title: Graph (client.graph)
graph.description: Node, edge, cluster management and AI-based graph generation

restApi.title: REST API Endpoints
restApi.description: You can call REST APIs directly without the SDK. Base URL:

introPage.title: Introduction
introPage.description: GraphNode is a platform for visualizing and managing AI conversations as knowledge graphs. By understanding the context and relationships between conversations, you can gain better insights.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: GraphNode goes beyond simply storing conversations with AI assistants - it analyzes and visualizes the connections between conversations. This allows users to understand their thought patterns and explore related topics.

aiChat.title: AI Chat
aiChat.description: Chat with various AI models including OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, and DeepSeek. Streaming support allows real-time response viewing.

knowledgeGraph.title: Knowledge Graph
knowledgeGraph.description: Conversations are automatically converted to nodes, and related conversations are connected by edges. Similar topics are grouped through clustering.

mcpExtension.title: MCP Extension
mcpExtension.description: Through Model Context Protocol, AI can access external tools. Integration with GitHub, databases, file systems, and more is possible.

noteManagement.title: Note Management
noteManagement.description: Organize insights from conversations into notes and folders. Markdown is supported.

realtimeSync.title: Real-time Sync
realtimeSync.description: All data is synchronized to the cloud in real-time. Access the same data across multiple devices.

step1.title: Download App
step1.description: Download the desktop app for Windows, macOS, or Linux.

step2.title: Google Login
step2.description: Sign in with your Google account to get started.

step3.title: Start Conversation
step3.description: Select your preferred AI model and start a conversation. Conversations are automatically converted to graphs.

step4.title: Explore Graph
step4.description: Visually explore the connections between conversations in the graph view.

support.title: Support
support.description: If you encounter issues or have questions, please use the channels below.

cli.title: GraphNode CLI
cli.description: The GraphNode CLI is a terminal-friendly companion tool for creating and inspecting SQLite-backed notes from the command line. It also exposes runtime checks and legacy file-note access without opening the desktop app.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: The CLI ships as the @graphnode/cli package inside the GraphNode Front repository. It complements the Electron app by working against the same GraphNode home directory and SQLite note storage.
overview.feature1: Create and search SQLite-backed notes from your terminal
overview.feature2: Inspect local runtime and bootstrap state with doctor and sqlite commands
overview.feature3: Resolve note ids, print note contents, open previews, and soft-delete notes
overview.feature4: Access legacy file-backed notes through the file-note commands

quickstart.title: Quick Start
quickstart.description: Run the CLI directly via npm without a global install:
quickstart.note: SQLite notes are stored in ~/.graphnode/graphnode.db by default, while legacy file notes remain available in ~/.graphnode/cli/notes.

install.title: Global Install
install.description: For local development, link the package so you can run graphnode from anywhere:
install.note: This is only for local development. End users should not need npm link.

distribution.title: Distribution Options
distribution.description: Based on the latest CLI README in GraphNode Front, the current distribution plan covers three paths.
distribution.option1: npm package: best when GraphNode CLI is shipped as a standalone developer tool. Run graphnode:release-check before publish to verify the standalone bundle.
distribution.option2: Homebrew: best for macOS-focused distribution. Users can install with brew install graphnode and immediately run graphnode help.
distribution.option3: App bundle included CLI: best when the desktop installer should also ship the CLI. Release builds already bundle the CLI resources, but exposing graphnode on PATH is still platform-specific.

commands.title: Commands
commands.description: All available commands and their usage.
commands.helpDesc: Prints the full list of available commands and usage examples.
commands.doctorDesc: Displays local runtime information including platform, Node.js version, home directory, working directory, GraphNode home, and the legacy notes directory path.
commands.noteDesc: Create, inspect, search, open, and delete notes stored in SQLite.
commands.noteQuery: Use <query> as either an exact note ID or a search term contained in the note title/body. Notes created by the current CLI use a cli-timestamp ID format.
commands.noteAdd: note add <title> — Create a new SQLite note
commands.noteStorage: SQLite notes are stored in ~/.graphnode/graphnode.db by default. Legacy file notes remain available in ~/.graphnode/cli/notes through the file-note commands, and you can override the storage root with GRAPHNODE_HOME.
commands.demoDesc: Runs a small demo workflow covering terminal note creation, the shared GraphNode home directory, and SQLite startup sync inspection.

home.title: Alex, what should we think through?
home.subtitle: Open a note, ask the agent, or explore your graph.
home.placeholder: Ask GraphNode to summarize your notes, review a conversation, or build a graph from recent context...

chat.placeholder: Ask GraphNode to summarize notes, search threads, or generate a graph from your latest conversations...

messages.text: Summarize the key themes repeating in this week's workspace.

Three threads stand out this week.

- Connecting notes and the graph more naturally in the experience
- Making agent output clearer — sources and reasoning both visible
- Helping first-time users understand workspace structure faster

Each one points to the same underlying need: the app should feel coherent from the first interaction, not just powerful once you know it.
Turn those into product-level action items.

Here are three concrete next steps:

1. **Entry point for first note → graph** — make it obvious how a note becomes a connected node. One clear affordance, not buried in menus.
2. **Source chips on agent responses** — every answer should show which notes or conversations it drew from, clickable to jump directly.
3. **Home screen clarity** — the three modes (notes, chat, graph) need short, visible roles so a new user understands what to open first.

Want me to draft copy or spec any of these?
Which of the three should we ship first?

Start with source chips. It's the most contained change — it touches every agent response without requiring a layout redesign.

Users who already understand notes and chat will see the value immediately. It also makes the other two feel more urgent once people realize how much context the agent already has.

The note → graph entry point is higher leverage but needs more design exploration. Tackle it after you've shipped chips and seen how users respond.
What should the first release include?

Three pieces are enough for the first release.

- Source chips above each answer
- Clickable jumps back to the note or thread
- A small row of follow-up actions below the response

That already makes the agent feel grounded in real workspace context.
And what comes right after that?

Open the note → graph entry point next.

If people can see connected structure right after writing a note, GraphNode's core value becomes much easier to understand. After that, tighten the home onboarding copy so the whole flow feels even clearer.
